channel.title: Geopolitics on DocB — Timeline
channel.description: Recent content in Geopolitics on DocB — Timeline

item.title: Rassvet: Russia's Answer to SpaceX's Kill Switch
item.description: On May 6, Kyivstar became Ukraine's authorized Starlink reseller for enterprises, schools, and hospitals. The whitelist that disabled Russian terminals and the dealership that channels Ukrainian ones are one piece of infrastructure seen from two ends.

item.title: Your 3.5% Amazon Surcharge Began in a Texas Control Room
item.description: A Valero hydrotreater explosion at 98% refinery utilization eliminated America's last diesel buffer — and the cost landed on every Amazon seller's dashboard.

item.title: 33.5% Full: Inside Europe's Storage Gamble
item.description: A Madrid control room operator intervenes every fifteen minutes while LNG cargoes vanish and gas turbines prop up a solar grid that can't stabilise itself

item.title: The Cancellation Clause That Closed a Strait
item.description: How Lloyd's 72-hour war-risk withdrawal handed Iran a $2 million per-tanker toll at Hormuz — and European ports started requiring proof of payment

item.title: Iran Built a Velvet Rope Around 21% of Global Oil
item.description: Flag-state discrimination converts Hormuz from a commons to a permission gate — and the IRGC is collecting the toll

item.title: We're Bolting Dummy Weights Into Our Most Advanced Fighters
item.description: F-35 Lot 17 delivered without radars. 11,000 munitions burned in 16 days. The US defence industrial base is failing at quality and surge simultaneously.

item.title: Hungary's €430-Per-Tonne Election Strategy
item.description: Hormuz → EU gas storage at 28.9% → fertilizer costs surge → Hungary's electoral cycle turns supply shock into sanctions defection vector

item.title: The Pentagon Has 60 Days of Rare Earths Left
item.description: PLA purges narrow China's coercive toolkit — leaving rare earth export controls as the one weapon that requires no command chain integrity

item.title: The Gas That Thinks
item.description: Hormuz closes, Qatar's helium halts, the Federal Reserve depletes — and AI scaling hits a ceiling made of noble gas
